# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that runs directly on the master node for faster execution. The pipeline performs builds, runs tests, and deploys to production. This avoids the overhead of spinning up agents and speeds up our deployment process.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline { 
    agent any // run Jenkins on all nodes - no need for master node here as it is already configured in JENKINS_URL/jnlpJars/agent.jar, so we only use the slave machines to execute pipeline code and don't have an overall management of these tasks like agents
    stages { 
        stage('Build'){ // The build step should be triggered when changes are pushed into your repository or manually run by a human user (build request)   - Jenkinsfile is responsible for this. A change in the .git/config file can trigger an execution of 'master' pipeline again which runs only if there were any unchecked-out files and no staged, committed changed
            steps{ 
                // Define your build tasks here (could be calling gradle or mvn commands) - Jenkinsfile is responsible for this. Use the jenkins/golang:1 directory in Docker to run Golang related jobs    You should replace 'GO_MODULE' with Go module name, and define steps inside
                // For example if your project has go modules named `api` then you will use that here  - Jenkinsfile is responsible for this. Use the jenkins/golaugmented:1 directory in Docker to run Golang related jobs    You should replace 'GO_MODULE' with Go module name, and define steps inside
                // For example if your project has go modules named `db` then you will use that here   - Jenkinsfile is responsible for this. Use the jenkins/golaugmented:1 directory in Docker to run Golang related jobs    You should replace 'GO_MODULE' with Go module name, and define steps inside
            } 
        } // end of Build stage       It also has a trigger rule set which is defined through Jenkinfile. The build step runs on changes made into .git/config or when manually run by the user (build request)   - By default no stages will be triggered unless this section in Jobs gets updated with tasks to execute   
        
        stage('Test'){  // Define your test steps here, could either call integration tests using Golang modules named `api` and another for db. Jenkinsfile is responsible for the definition of these jobs (golaugmented:1 directory)   - This should be executed on a machine that has ginkgo installed to run unit or end-to-end test cases
            steps{  // If you are using Docker, replace `docker` with your specific container name. Jenkinsfile is responsible for this and it also sets up the necessary environment variables inside docker jobs   - This should be executed in a separate machine that has all dependencies required to run tests installed (this may vary by language)
                // Define steps here as per requirement – For instance if you have `db` module, then define test cases for db. Jenkinsfile is responsible this part and it sets the necessary environment variables inside Docker jobs   - This should be executed in a separate machine that has all dependencies required to run tests installed (this may vary by language)
            }  // end of Test stage        It also triggers whenever there's an updated .git/config file or when manually triggered through Jenkinfile. The test step runs on any change made into the `db` module, and if a build is successful it will run integration tests   - By default no stages will be executed unless this section in Jobs gets defined with tasks to execute
         } // end of Stage block – If there are more than one staging/deploying steps or branches you can add them inside any given stage. The execution step runs on changes pushed into .git directories, trigger by Jenkinfile and also manually run as per requirement   - By default no stages will be triggered unless this section in Jobs gets updated with tasks to execute
      } // end of Stages block – All staged jobs are grouped inside these blocks. If there’s only one task that needs execution it can go directly at the root or top stage, otherwise each job should have its own associated branch   - By default no stages will be executed unless this section in Jobs gets defined with tasks to execute
    } // end of Pipeline block – This is for setting global steps inside Jenkinsfile. There's a set of conditional checks that determine whether the code runs or not if you have more blocks, branches etc Inside these conditionals   - By default no stages will be executed unless this section in Jobs gets defined with tasks to execute
} // end of Pipeline block – This is for setting global steps inside Jenkinsfile. There's a set of conditional checks that determine whether the code runs or not if you have more blocks, branches etc Inside these conditionals   - By default no stages will be executed unless this section in Jobs gets defined with tasks to execute
